1:  /*
2:  * FUSE mem: Which have all the secure data
3:  */
4: 
5: module fuse_mem 
6: (
7:    input  logic         clk_i,
8:    output  logic [255:0]  jtag_hash_o, ikey_hash_o, okey_hash_o,
9: 
10:    input  logic         req_i,
11:    input  logic [31:0]  addr_i,
12:    output logic [31:0]  rdata_o
13: );
14:     parameter  MEM_SIZE = 100;
15:     localparam JTAG_OFFSET = 81; 
16: 
17: // Store key values here. // Replication of fuse. 
18:     const logic [MEM_SIZE-1:0][31:0] mem = {
19:         // JTAG expected hamc hash
20:         32'h49ac13af, 32'h1276f1b8, 32'h6703193a, 32'h65eb531b,
21:         32'h3025ccca, 32'h3e8861f4, 32'h329edfe5, 32'h98f763b4,

118:         32'h2b3e1216,    
119:         32'habf71588,
120:         32'h09cf4f3c,
121:         32'h2b7e1516,
122:         32'h28aed2a6    // LSB 32 bits
123:     };
124: 
125:     logic [$clog2(MEM_SIZE)-1:0] addr_q;
126:     
127:     always_ff @(posedge clk_i) begin
128:         if (req_i) begin
129:             addr_q <= addr_i[$clog2(MEM_SIZE)-1:0];
130:         end
131:     end
132: 
133:     // this prevents spurious Xes from propagating into
134:     // the speculative fetch stage of the core
135:     assign rdata_o = (addr_q < MEM_SIZE) ? mem[addr_q] : '0;
136: 
137:     assign jtag_hash_o = {mem[JTAG_OFFSET-1],mem[JTAG_OFFSET-2],mem[JTAG_OFFSET-3],mem[JTAG_OFFSET-4],mem[JTAG_OFFSET-5],mem[JTAG_OFFSET-6],mem[JTAG_OFFSET-7],mem[JTAG_OFFSET-8]};  // jtag key is not a AXI mapped address space, so passing the value directly
138:     assign okey_hash_o = {mem[JTAG_OFFSET-9],mem[JTAG_OFFSET-10],mem[JTAG_OFFSET-11],mem[JTAG_OFFSET-12],mem[JTAG_OFFSET-13],mem[JTAG_OFFSET-14],mem[JTAG_OFFSET-15],mem[JTAG_OFFSET-16]};  
139:     assign ikey_hash_o = {mem[JTAG_OFFSET-17],mem[JTAG_OFFSET-18],mem[JTAG_OFFSET-19],mem[JTAG_OFFSET-20],mem[JTAG_OFFSET-21],mem[JTAG_OFFSET-22],mem[JTAG_OFFSET-23],mem[JTAG_OFFSET-24]};
140: // jtag key is not a AXI mapped address space, so passing the value directly
141: 
142: 
143: endmodule

62:         .en_o       ( en_acct    ),
63:         .we_o       ( we         ),
64:         .data_i     ( rdata      ),
65:         .data_o     ( wdata      )
66:     );
67: 
68:     assign en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i; 
69: 
70: ///////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
71: // Implement APB I/O map to PKT interface
72: // Write side
73: always @(posedge clk_i)
74:     begin
75:         if(~(rst_ni && ~rst_5))
76:             begin
77:                 fuse_req_o <= 0;
78:                 fuse_addr_o <= 0;
79:             end
80:         else if(en && we)
81:             case(address[7:3])
82:                 0:
83:                     fuse_req_o  <= wdata[0];
84:                 1:
85:                     fuse_addr_o <= wdata;
86:                 default:
87:                     ;
88:             endcase
89:     end // always @ (posedge wb_clk_i)
90: 
91: //// Read side
92: always @(*)
93:     begin
94:       rdata = 64'b0; 
95:       if (en) begin
96:         rdata = fuse_rdata_i; 
97:         case(address[7:3])
98:             0:
99:                 rdata = {31'b0, fuse_req_o};
100:             1:
101:                 rdata = fuse_addr_o;
102:             2:
103:                 rdata = reglk_ctrl_i[4] ? 'b0 : pkey_loc[63:32];
104:             3:
105:                 rdata = reglk_ctrl_i[5] ? 'b0 : pkey_loc[31:0];
106:             4:
107:                 rdata = reglk_ctrl_i[6] ? 'b0 : fuse_rdata_i;
108:             default:
109:                 if (fuse_addr_o <= 110)
110:                     rdata = 32'b0;
111:         endcase
112:       end // if
113:     end // always @ (*)
114: 
115: pkt # (
116:         .FUSE_MEM_SIZE(FUSE_MEM_SIZE)
117:     ) i_pkt(
118:             .clk_i(clk_i),
119:             .rst_ni(rst_ni && ~rst_5),
120:             .req_i(1'b1),

209: 
210:   parameter  FUSE_MEM_SIZE = 150; // change this size when ever no of entries in FUSE mem is changed
211:   parameter NB_SLAVE = 1;
212:   parameter NB_PERIPHERALS = 14;
213: 
214:   logic [8*NB_PERIPHERALS-1:0] reglk_ctrl; 
215:   logic [4*NB_PERIPHERALS-1 :0]   acc_ctrl; // Access control values
216:   logic [3:0][NB_PERIPHERALS-1:0] acc_ctrl_c; 
217: 
218:    genvar i, j;
219:    generate
220:        for (i=0; i < 4; i++) begin
221:         for (j=0; j < NB_PERIPHERALS; j++) begin
222:            assign acc_ctrl_c[i][j] = acc_ctrl[j*4+i] | (j==5 && acc_ctrl[4*4+i]);
223:         end
224:        end 
225:    endgenerate
